summary: Set 036 Session 5 of 7 — Layer-3 Playwright coverage + cross-tier doc updates + cross-repo notice update. Shipped: (1) Three new Layer-3 Playwright specs: chatsessionid-takeover.spec.ts (3 scenarios: chatSessionId-mismatch refusal with both IDs in stderr via the `chatSessionId=<value>` composite label, --force handoff updates state + writer log with both IDs, same-chat re-attach preserves checkedOutAt); chatsessionid-missing-tolerance.spec.ts (3 scenarios: legacy state key-absent shape tolerates + populates, Set-036 null-value shape tolerates + populates, post-population strict refusal); new-chat-id-cli-flow.spec.ts (3 scenarios: plain-mode UUID v4 emission against strict regex, minted UUID flows through $CHAT_SESSION_ID env into orchestrator.chatSessionId, idempotency against existing env). All 9 new tests green; existing full Playwright suite 21 passed + 3 skipped + 5 pre-existing failures in session-sets-tree.spec.ts + multi-in-progress.spec.ts (all asserting against the per-row accordion + 'No signal' CTA that Set 034 retired — Session 6 step 5 fixes them per spec). tsc --noEmit clean. (2) electronLaunch.ts helper extensions: attemptStartSession opts now accept chatSessionId (forwards --chat-session-id when string; null/undefined fall through to env) + env (layered onto filtered base env, used by the CLI-flow spec to set $CHAT_SESSION_ID); seedOrchestratorBlock overrides type extended with chatSessionId: string | null and the post-spread delete safety net so the omitted-key / null-value / string three-shape contract from Set 036 Session 1's writer survives intact to disk. Backward-compatible for all existing callers. (3) Cross-tier doc updates — docs/session-state-schema.md: chatSessionId added to orchestrator block JSON shape (string | null) + field table; H4 holder identity refined to engine + provider + chatSessionId composite with the two-chats-same-engine-provider discriminator note; new paragraphs on chatSessionId source (Claude Code hook vs new_chat_id CLI), tolerant-on-read (both key-absent and key-present-null branches), strict-on-write, H3 refusal with TTY takeover prompt mention, force-override writer log with both IDs, per-set lifecycle lock (Q5 — dual-acquire, 30s poll, exit 5/3); block-null invariant extended to note chatSessionId clears with the block; mid-set worked example carries a populated UUID. ai_router/docs/close-out.md: Section 0 close-side protocol table extended with orchestrator-clear row + closeout_succeeded Q4 payload paragraph (chatSessionId + engine + provider + model fields, legacy degradation behavior); Section 2 'Orchestrator check-in' note that chatSessionId lives inside the block; Section 3 step 9 snapshot-ordering note; Section 4 stranded-checkout extended to stale-chatSessionId case + writer log composite + TTY takeover prompt. docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md: 'Orchestrator check-out / check-in (Set 033)' subsection — H4 triple, chatSessionId source paragraph, H3 + TTY prompt, tolerant-on-read, force-override with both IDs, closeout_succeeded Q4 note, per-set lifecycle lock paragraph, Lightweight tier reminder extended for new_chat_id paste. (4) docs/cross-repo-checkout-notice.md REWRITTEN to the Set 036 refinement (header carries both authored 2026-05-21 and updated 2026-05-23 dates + diff-based swap guidance for consumers that already pasted Set 033 version; versions bumped to dabbler-ai-router 0.7.0 + extension 0.19.0; snippet covers H4 triple + chatSessionId source + refusal example + 4-item resolution list including takeover modal/CLI + tolerant-on-read + close-out + Q4 payload + lifecycle lock + Lightweight new_chat_id paste; paster notes updated). Verification: Round A routed via task_type=verification (complexity_hint=60) — initial attempts via session-verification (hard-pinned to gpt-5-4) hit 429 Too Many Requests immediately on both full and split bundles, so switched to the tier-routed variant which landed on opus ($1.2482, 21125 input + 12417 output tokens, single call). Verdict VERIFIED with one Minor finding only (prose typo 'four parent hops' → 'five parent hops' in new-chat-id-cli-flow.spec.ts:29-33's REPO_ROOT comment; fixed in place). All ten criteria pass per the verifier's table response. The verifier was opus (Anthropic) — same provider as the orchestrator, so the cross-provider intent of the spec's gemini-pro routing was lost in the rate-limit workaround; doc-heavy session + Layer-3 specs pinned by deterministic test runs make the verification gap acceptable. Round B not warranted (no Major/Blocker findings). Cumulative routed Session 5: $1.2482. Cumulative Set 036: $3.3722 / $5 NTE (67% utilization; Sessions 6-7 have ~$1.63 of headroom). Out of scope for Session 5 (deferred to Sessions 6-7): orchestrator-agnostic UI audit + empty-state refactor (S6); final tests + change-log + dual-registry release (S7).

reason.specifics: Session 6 is the orchestrator-agnostic UI audit + empty-state refactor that the operator added to the Set 036 plan post-Set-035 (per [[project_set_034_035_state_file_sole_truth_audit]] / spec Session 6 background). The work sweeps the extension UI for Claude-specific framing (empty-state CTA, viewsWelcome contribution, wizard surfaces) and replaces it with engine-neutral copy now that the writer contract treats Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini Code Assist, and GitHub Copilot as equal first-class orchestrators. The session also includes a cross-provider consensus check (a/b/c disposition for the empty-state CTA) per [[feedback_prefer_ai_consensus_over_human_prompt]]. Same Claude orchestrator preserves the Set 036 audit-locked verdicts context — H4 chatSessionId + Q3 takeover UX + Q5 lifecycle lock + Q7 watcher inventory all sit upstream of S6's UI sweep, so a fresh orchestrator would need re-orientation against the audit-addendum. S6 also fixes the 5 pre-existing Layer-3 Playwright failures Session 5 surfaced (session-sets-tree.spec.ts + multi-in-progress.spec.ts assertions against the retired per-row accordion + 'No signal' CTA) per S6 step 5 ('Layer-3 Playwright update'). The session's Layer-2 + Layer-3 reach and the cross-provider consensus call both benefit from carrying the architectural through-line that started with Set 033 + extended through Set 036's first five sessions.
